system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    // Vertices 0-7
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Vertices 8-11
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    // Vertices 12-15
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - extended outlet duct
    // Vertices 16-19
    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight section
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Long outlet straight section
    hex (15 16 17 14 19 18 17 14) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (7 11 10 6)
            (8 9 6 3)

            // Block 2 walls
            (11 12 13 10)
            (3 15 14 2)
            (11 15 14 10)
            (12 13 2 3)

            // Block 3 walls
            (15 16 17 14)
            (19 18 17 14)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (16 17 17 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

{
    version     2.0;

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 51.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

The primary error is:
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the hex blocks does not follow the right-hand rule convention required by OpenFOAM. When viewed from outside the block, the vertices should go around the face in a counter-clockwise direction.

### Root Cause

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the vertex ordering in several blocks is incorrect. For example:

**Block 0:** `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` — This winding produces a negative volume (inside-out).

**Block 1:** `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` — Also likely wrong.

**Block 2:** `hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)` — Same issue.

**Block 3:** Has repeated/misordered vertices (`(16 17 17 14)`) which is definitely wrong.

Additionally, the **boundary face definitions** need to be updated to match the new correct block topology after fixing the blocks.

### Solution

Redefine the blocks so that each hex definition follows the correct right-hand rule. Looking at the squareBend tutorial's blockMeshDict structure, the typical approach is:

For a hex block `(v0 v1 v2 v3)(i j k)`, the first four vertices form one face and the last four form the opposite face, with consistent winding.

A corrected `blockMeshDict` would have something like:

```
// Block 0: Inlet straight section
hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

// Block 1: Bend section
hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

// Block 2: Post-bend straight section
hex (5 12 13 9 6 15 14 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

// Block 3: Extended outlet duct
hex (6 16 17 12 7 19 18 15) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

And the **boundary patches** would also need to be redefined to match these corrected block boundaries. Each wall face should reference the actual external-facing sides of the corrected blocks.

After rewriting `blockMeshDict`, run `blockMesh` again before running `rhoSimpleFoam`.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed (Error 1 above), no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, etc.). Once `blockMesh` runs successfully and the mesh is created, this error will be resolved automatically.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Patch Names Mismatch

The `blockMeshDict` defines boundary patches named `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, but the boundary condition files (`0/T`, `0/U`, `0/k`, `0/e`, `0/nut`) reference patches named `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`. These appear consistent across most files, but verify that **all** 0/ field files use exactly the same patch names as defined in `blockMeshDict`. If any file uses different names (e.g., `walls` instead of individual named walls), that would cause additional problems.

Also note that the `0/p` file references `inlet` and `outlet` patches, while `blockMeshDict` defines `inlet` and `outlet` — this is correct. But some other field files may still be using generic names like `walls` instead of the specific ones from `blockMeshDict`. Ensure consistency across all 0/ files.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Fix Block 0 from hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) to hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) maintaining same grading; Fix Block 1 from hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) to hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6) maintaining same grading; Fix Block 2 from hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) to hex (5 12 13 9 6 15 14 10) maintaining same grading; Fix Block 3 from hex (15 16 17 14 19 18 17 14) to hex (6 16 17 12 7 19 18 15) with proper vertex ordering and grading (40 10 10); Update all boundary face definitions to match corrected block topology - inlet face remains (0 1 2 3), outlet face becomes (16 17 18 19), and update all wall faces to reflect the new correct block boundaries where each face follows the right-hand rule convention'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
